{{Quote|Coruscant can be a hard world.|Captain Yur T'aug|<ref name="JA:TDH" />}}
'''Yur T'aug''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] who lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. T'aug served as a [[Police officer/Legends|police officer]] with the [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Security Force]] (CSF) and, by [[43 BBY/Legends|43 BBY]], had achieved the [[Police rank|rank]] of [[Captain/Legends|captain]]. T'aug was in charge of the [[Inspector/Legends|investigations]] into the [[Death/Legends|homicides]] of [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Uta S'orn]]'s son, [[Ren S'orn]], and a petty [[Scoundrel/Legends|thief]] and [[Information broker/Legends|informant]] named [[Fligh]]. Both were the results of several killings on Coruscant in which the victims were drained of [[Blood/Legends|blood]] before being killed. Throughout the investigation, T'aug clashed with [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] on how the CSF was treating the cases.

==Biography==
{{Quote|This is not the first body we've found drained of blood. Drifter, lowlifes—beings no one would miss. Over the past year, there have been a half dozen. Maybe more we haven't found. Who knows?|Yur T'aug, to Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn|<ref name="JA:TDH" />}}
Yur T'aug was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] who served as a [[Police officer/Legends|police officer]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. By [[43 BBY/Legends|43 BBY]], T'aug had achieved the [[Police rank|rank]] of [[Captain/Legends|captain]] and worked as an [[Inspector/Legends|inspector]] investigating cases such as [[Death/Legends|homicide]].<ref name="JA:TDH" />

In 43 BBY, the murdered body of [[Ren S'orn]], son of [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Uta S'orn]], was discovered on the planet [[Simpla-12]]. His body had been drained of [[Blood/Legends|blood]] in a manner similar to several cases on Coruscant. Because of that, T'aug was sent to investigate the murder. However, T'aug dropped the case at the request of Senator S'orn, Ren S'orn's [[Parent/Legends|mother]]. Ren was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] but had left his [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] with the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and had become a loner. Senator S'orn feared what T'aug may have discovered and simply wanted the matter to go away.<ref name="JA:TEE">''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Evil Experiment]]''</ref>

However, six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] later, T'aug was called to investigate the death of [[Fligh]], who had been found in the [[Lane of All Worlds]], an alleyway next to Coruscant's [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Building]]. Fligh was a [[Scoundrel/Legends|petty thief]] but also an [[Information broker/Legends|informant]] for [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]]. T'aug discovered that Fligh's body had been drained of blood in a way similar to the earlier cases. [[Didi Oddo]], a friend of Jinn's, informed the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] of Fligh's murder. When Jinn and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] apprentice, [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], went to the crime scene and asked to see the body, T'aug consented. After some questioning by the Jedi, T'aug reluctantly revealed information about the case. He told the Jedi of the previous killings in which the bodies had been drained of blood. However, he did not inform them of the connection to Ren S'orn.<ref name="JA:TDH" /> As it later turned out, Fligh had faked his own death in order to escape to the planet [[Belasco/Legends|Belasco]] after he had stolen Senator S'orn's [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]].<ref name="JA:TDR">''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Dangerous Rescue]]''</ref>

Jinn and Kenobi looked into the case further, discovering that T'aug had failed to properly investigate Ren's death. Kenobi confronted T'aug, who, though refusing to answer at first, admitted it had been at the Senator's request that the case had been dropped. The Jedi later discovered that Ren had been murdered by [[Jenna Zan Arbor]], a [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] who had conducted experiments on Ren before killing him. Arbor had also been responsible for the other deaths in which the victims had been drained of blood.<ref name="JA:TEE" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I should be investigating, not filling you in. You can read the report when I am done.|Yur T'aug, to Qui-Gon Jinn|<ref name="JA:TDH" />}}
Yur T'aug was a stockily built Bothan who had a flowing beard and glossy dark [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. Though T'aug was very professional in handling his job as a police officer, he was not especially happy with outside assistance from the Jedi. He resented that as police he was required to honor any requests by Jedi and to let them move in on his investigations. To a certain degree he was willing to work with them on a professional level and so outwardly accepted their suggestions, though internally he had a lack of interest in any of their tips or observations.<ref name="JA:TDH" /> T'aug especially disliked the Jedi questioning his motives or actions on other cases. When questioned by the Jedi on the handling of the Ren S'orn case, T'aug openly and rudely refused to cooperate, even though the CSF was mandated to do so. Only when Obi-Wan Kenobi reminded T'aug that both the police and the Jedi were on the side of justice did T'aug reluctantly tell the Jedi of the facts surrounding the S'orn case.<ref name="JA:TEE" />

==Behind the scenes==
Yur T'aug first appeared in the [[2000]] young-readers novel ''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Deadly Hunter]]'', written by [[Jude Watson]] and published on [[December 1]], [[2000]].<ref name="JA:TDH" />
